applications.name: MK_editor
applications.purpose: Optional desktop editor shell built by the dependency-free `desktop-editor` lane. The previous SDL3/Dear ImGui shell has been replaced in the active `MK_editor` target by a clean Win32 first-party retained shell owned privately under `editor/src`, with `MK_editor_core` remaining the supported editor logic and AI-operable retained-model surface for project IO, authoring, core-backed `EditorDockLayout` / `editor_dock_panel_catalog` dock and panel identity rows, value-only `EditorDockCommandKind` / `EditorDockCommandRequest` / `EditorDockCommandPlan` dock mutation planners, core-backed `EditorRichText*` document/selection/copy/view-model/AI rows, profiler rows, generated game studio rows, input rebinding rows, play-in-editor review rows, game-module driver tests, material preview retained rows, and the first native panel set. The shell renders Main Menu, Scene, Inspector, Assets, Console, Viewport, Resources, AI Commands, Profiler, Timeline, and Project Settings panels from the core dock layout/catalog stored on `Workspace` through `mirakana::ui` documents and `MK_ui_renderer` submissions, binds private Win32 file-dialog, clipboard, reviewed process-runner, Windows SDK DirectWrite text-layout/glyph-raster service adapters, and a private Windows TSF text-input/IME session adapter selected through first-party UI contracts, and reports deterministic `editor_shell_ui=first_party`, `editor_shell_backend=d3d12`, `editor_shell_imgui=0`, `editor_shell_panels=11`, `editor_shell_docking_status=single_window_ready`, `editor_shell_dock_tab_headers=11`, `editor_shell_dock_split_gutters=3`, `editor_shell_dock_active_panels=4`, `editor_shell_dock_focusable_controls=11`, `editor_shell_sdl3=0`, `editor_shell_file_dialog_service=win32`, `editor_shell_clipboard_service=win32`, `editor_shell_reviewed_process_runner=win32`, `editor_shell_viewport_status=diagnostic_only`, `editor_shell_viewport_native_handles_exposed=0`, `editor_shell_material_preview_status=diagnostic_only`, `editor_shell_material_preview_native_handles_exposed=0`, `editor_shell_text_atlas_handoff_status=glyphs_ready_atlas_handoff_host_gated`, `editor_shell_text_font_adapter_invoked=1`, `editor_shell_text_font_glyphs_ready=1`, `editor_shell_text_font_fallback_used=0`, `editor_shell_text_atlas_handoff_host_gated_rows=1`, `editor_shell_text_atlas_handoff_unsupported_rows=1`, `editor_shell_text_font_native_handles_exposed=0`, `editor_shell_text_input_service=win32_tsf`, `editor_shell_ime_service=win32_tsf`, `editor_shell_ime_status=win32_tsf_selected`, `editor_shell_ime_text_input_session_rows=0`, `editor_shell_ime_composition_rows=0`, `editor_shell_ime_committed_text_rows=0`, `editor_shell_ime_caret_rect_rows=1`, `editor_shell_ime_surrounding_text_rows=1`, `editor_shell_ime_candidate_ui_host_owned=1`, `editor_shell_ime_native_handles_exposed=0`, `editor_shell_renderer_boxes_submitted`, and `editor_shell_renderer_text_runs_available` smoke counters. `main_menu` is shell chrome in the catalog, while `input_rebinding` remains a workspace panel outside the current native shell panel set. Dock command planning and AI operation overloads can inspect dock layout rows, read rich_text_rows, and show, hide, activate, move, split, and reset tab-stack layouts through reviewed dry-run/apply rows; clean-break GameEngine.Workspace.v2 persists dock graph rows through MK_editor_core text-store IO. The shell now renders single-window dock tab headers, disabled hidden tabs, split gutters, active/focused panel state, keyboard focus traversal over dock tabs, Console diagnostics, AI Commands status/command/evidence rows, Inspector property rows as read-only rich-text spans, and a focused Project Settings text field with private Windows TSF session selection, first-party caret rect, surrounding text, composition, commit, cancel, strict cursor/selection validation, and fail-closed adapter dispatch rows; it validates selected DirectWrite text shaping and glyph rasterization through existing `ITextShapingAdapter` / `IFontRasterizerAdapter` contracts and records selected text atlas handoff evidence rows without exposing native handles. Multi-window docking drag/tear-off, help rich text, broad editable rich text, Direct2D GPU text rendering/upload, broad shaping/bidi/fallback, full app-owned ITextStoreACP callback coverage, native IME candidate UI, reconversion, accessibility publication, and cross-platform font adapters remain later private shell phases. The Viewport and Material Preview surfaces are diagnostic-only until later private D3D12 texture adapters own offscreen render targets, barriers, descriptor leases, material uploads, and resize-safe teardown inside `editor/src`; material-preview GPU parity remains unsupported while diagnostic-only. `tools/build-editor.ps1` configures, builds, and tests the current native editor lane, and `tools/evaluate-cpp23.ps1 -Editor` covers the current C++23 editor preset. The shell must not depend on SDL3, Dear ImGui, Qt, Slint, RmlUi, or UI middleware, and must not expose Win32, D3D12, DXGI, DirectWrite, TSF, COM, swapchain, descriptor heap, clipboard, process, file-dialog, texture, or UI-middleware handles through public editor, engine, gameplay, or runtime UI APIs.
